subject: aaf auto cert generation
commit_message:
aaf auto cert generation

Add support for AAF init container for config and cert generation

Issue-ID: SDNC-755
Signed-off-by: Agarwal, Ruchira (ra1926) <ra1926@att.com>
Change-Id: I06ee7921b6dbb1b4b9ca64cf276a374256af3a45
Signed-off-by: Agarwal, Ruchira (ra1926) <ra1926@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

Hello,
I do not see the ability to optionally disable AAF integration. This is expected as part of the M# security checklist. Other applications such as AAI, CLAMP already have a configuration value to do this. Since this does not yet exist in this helm chart please refer to  https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-1986 for the general global configuration value we would like to align all projects to.

Thanks,

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

@Ruchira, have you seen my comment?

Patch Set 1:

@Sylvain - yes thanks, will try to circle back to this and support the disable AAF functionality soon

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I still think that we shouldn’t see the aaf value especially in the global part. 
You should IMHO create an option in the config part to set the aaf endpoint (as an fqdn) and not set it per default. 

(Semi)hard coding the address will fool everybody blindly deploying your chart. 

And if your aaf server is out of the cluster, you set the option with the fqdn

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> I still think that we shouldn’t see the aaf value especially in the global part. 
> You should IMHO create an option in the config part to set the aaf endpoint (as an fqdn) and not set it per default. 
> 
> (Semi)hard coding the address will fool everybody blindly deploying your chart. 
> 
> And if your aaf server is out of the cluster, you set the option with the fqdn

Can you please provide a reference to someone that is integrated with AAF and following the model you suggest?  Because I believe we're doing the same thing here as AAI, which is one of the few projects to successfully integrate with AAF.
